name: Gravel road between Campo de Criptana and El Toboso
name_original: Ruta gravel entre Campo de Criptana y El Toboso
description:
What could be more quixotic than riding your bicycle along the gravel tracks that cross the La Mancha plain between Campo de Criptana and El Toboso? The Route that we propose shares a section with the complete route of the Don Quixote Route.

This tour takes you into the landscape of Cervantes' work, and takes you through an extensive steppe of reddish earth covered by a collage of olive groves, vineyards and other crops such as almonds or cereals. In addition, you pass through locations where you can contemplate the traditional white four-blade windmills that in the mind of the ingenious gentleman become giants. And, if that were not enough, you pass through typical towns in La Mancha, which have a traditional configuration, apart from the possibility of tasting local products.

The adventure is circular from Campo de Criptana, where you can reach by train or by road. The distance of the journey is affordable, even more so if you take into account that you only ascend 300 meters. For this reason, the route does not represent any technical or physical difficulty, so a good gravel bike is enough to ride with guarantees. The roads of La Mancha are perfect for pedaling on the dry days of spring and autumn. Remember to bring supplies and spare parts for any eventuality and your photographic device on hand.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Campo de Criptana offers routes across the La Mancha plain, characterized by an extensive steppe of reddish earth. The terrain features a mix of olive groves, vineyards, and other crops, providing varied scenery. Elevation gains are generally manageable, with many routes featuring around 300 meters of ascent. The region's dry conditions in spring and autumn are suitable for cycling. Best gravel bike trails around Campo De Criptana The most popular gravel bike trail is Gravel road between Campo de Criptana and El Toboso, a 32.6 miles (52.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This route traverses the La Mancha plain, connecting two historic towns. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Campo de Criptana Windmills – Windmills of Campo de Criptana loop from Campo de Criptana, a moderate 40.3 miles (64.9 km) path. This route offers views of the iconic windmills and the surrounding agricultural landscape. Local gravel bikers also love the Laguna del Rey – Lagunas de Ruidera loop from Tomelloso, a 60.6 miles (97.5 km) trail leading through the Laguna del Rey area, often completed in about 8 hours 42 minutes. Gravel biking around Campo De Criptana is defined by the vast La Mancha plain, historic windmills, and agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. More than 40 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Campo De Criptana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in Campo de Criptana?
A: There are 15 gravel bike trails around Campo de Criptana, offering a range of options for different skill levels, from easy to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Campo de Criptana?
A: The gravel bike trails in Campo de Criptana primarily traverse the vast La Mancha plain. You'll encounter an extensive steppe of reddish earth, flanked by diverse agricultural landscapes including olive groves, vineyards, and fields of almonds and cereals. The terrain is generally not overly technical, with manageable elevation gains, making it accessible for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel routes in Campo de Criptana?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for families. The region's generally flat terrain and manageable elevation gains make many trails accessible. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Criptana Windmills loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that allows you to enjoy the iconic windmills without excessive difficulty.

Q: What are the best times of year for gravel biking in Campo de Criptana?
A: The dry conditions in spring and autumn are ideal for gravel biking in Campo de Criptana. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and stable weather, making for comfortable rides across the La Mancha plain.

Q: What notable landmarks or attractions can I see along the gravel bike trails?
A: Many routes offer views of the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2909432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;, often referred to as the 'Giants' from Don Quixote. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4658585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Veguilla&lt;/a&gt;, a significant wetland for migratory birds, or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3082656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Cristo de Villajos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Criptana Windmills loop from Campo de Criptana&lt;/a&gt;, which is 64.9 km long and offers great views of the windmills and surrounding agricultural landscape. Another longer option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31495727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Criptana Windmills loop from Quero&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of gravel biking trails in Campo de Criptana?
A: The trails around Campo de Criptana cater to various abilities. You'll find a mix of routes, with 1 classified as easy, 9 as moderate, and 5 as difficult. This variety ensures options for beginners looking for a gentle ride and more experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Is it possible to reach Campo de Criptana by public transport with a bike?
A: While specific public transport options for bikes can vary, Campo de Criptana is accessible by train, and some regional train services in Spain allow bicycles, often with specific conditions or at certain times. It's advisable to check with the local train operator (Renfe) for their current bicycle policy before planning your trip.

Q: Where can I park my car if I'm bringing my bike to Campo de Criptana?
A: Campo de Criptana, as a town, offers various public parking areas. You can typically find parking near the town center or at the base of the Sierra de los Molinos, where the windmills are located. These spots provide convenient access to the start of many gravel routes.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about the trails in Campo de Criptana?
A: Gravel bikers frequently praise the unique landscape of the La Mancha plain, especially the iconic windmills and the vast, open vistas. The relatively gentle terrain and the opportunity to explore the region immortalized by Don Quixote are also highly valued aspects of cycling here.

Q: Are there any longer gravel bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders looking for a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey – Lagunas de Ruidera loop from Tomelloso&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 97.5 km trail. This route leads through the Laguna del Rey area and typically takes around 8 hours and 42 minutes to complete, offering a more extensive exploration of the region.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat along the gravel routes?
A: While many routes traverse rural landscapes, Campo de Criptana itself and other nearby towns like El Toboso offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and find refreshments. It's a good idea to plan your route to pass through these towns or carry sufficient supplies for longer stretches.
tips: The most characteristic image of Campo de Criptana are the mills that are declared Assets of Cultural Interest, the Infanto, Sardinero and Burleta Mills are in turn declared Monuments of Historical and Artistic Interest.

The old ones, Sardinero, Burleta and Infante; Sovereigns of time and stories, they have been standing since the 16th century, preserving their original mechanism. The moderns, the Inca Garcilaso, the Cariari, the Vicente Huidobro, the Pilón, the Lagarto, the Culebro and the Poyatos are immense young men from 1900.

The Salicor lagoons are an important bird refuge in La Mancha. When they are at their peak, you can see flamingos and some migratory birds such as bustards or cranes. It is also common to see hawks hunting.

It can be said that the town of El Toboso is already recognized worldwide and its fame is due to the fact that according to the famous Cervantine work, "Dulcinea" lived here or better known as Aldonza Lorenzo, who was the lady of Don Quixote.
The town is very picturesque and its history not only revolves around the famous Quixote, it has also been of great importance in the history of Spain, being the place where the battle of Navas de Tolosa was fought in 1212 and later it will form part of the domain of the Order of Santiago.
Undoubtedly a town worth visiting with the whole family.
